Notice Of Motion; Motion To Compel Debtor Xue Li Yang'S Responses To Special Interrogatories Set One And Request For Production Of Documents Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions.
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
HaoHao Song, Esq., Klein Law Group, Tel. 415.693.9107, appearing for Plaintiff.
Leila Sokolov, Esq., Law Offices of Richard Wahng, Tel. 510.490.4447, appearing by Court Call for Defendant Sheng Li Chinese Massage LLC, et al.

Counsel stipulate to Judge Pro Tem David Mangini. Matter A R G U E D. Ruling: Creditor Shu Chun Wang's motion to compel debtor Xue Li Yang ("Yang") to respond to Special Interrogatories, Set One, and Requests for Production of Documents, Set One, is GRANTED. Yang is to provide objection-free written responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One, and Requests for Production of Documents, Set One and produce documents by no later than October 9, 2015. Sanctions against Yang and Yang's counsel are imposed in the amount of $500. Judge Pro Tem: David F. Mangini, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Not Reported. (302)

Creditor'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Judgment
Off Calendar Jun-18-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Haohao Song, of Klein Law Group, Tel. 415.693.9107, for plaintiffs;
Christopher Thomas, of Law Office of Richard Wahng, Tel. 510.491.5021, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: CREDITOR SHU WANG'S Motion To Amend Judgment is granted. The Labor Commissioner Award explicitly states that Plaintiff was employed by Shu Sheng Wang and Xue Li Yang, each individually AND as general partners of Meritouch, a general partnership. (Declaration of HaoHao Song, Ex. 1, Labor Commissioner Award, p. 2.) Accordingly, Wang and Yang are not individually liable solely because they are general partners in Meritouch. Rather, the Labor Commissioner found that Wang and Yang also employed Plaintiff in their capacity as individuals. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

MOTION TO AMEND/MODIFY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Feb-18-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. HAOHAO SONG of Klein Law Group, (415) 693-9107 appearing for Creditor Shu Chun Wang;
Atty. LEILA SOCKOLOV of Law Offices of Richard Wahng, Tel No. (510) 490-1100 appearing for Sheng Li Chinese Massage, LLC.

RULING - A R G U E D - PLAINTIFF SHU WANG'S MOTION TO AMEND/MODIFY JUDGMENT is granted. No timely opposition filed. The reply brief indicates that an opposition was served. The court has not record of the pleading being filed and the court did not receive a courtesy copy of the opposition in compliance with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. CREDITOR TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED AMENDED JUDGMENT TO THE COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
